mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals labor market tightening that compresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power with an F-statistic p-value above 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a supply-constrained regime reflects bottleneck pressures that reduce corporate output and trigger short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when INDPRO declines, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during identified supply bottleneck periods.

mechanism_narrative: Low policy rates combined with rising inflation erode real yields and push investors into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls increase aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of observations when PAYEMS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 1.0% signals severe demand destruction that forces corporate earnings downgrades and triggers negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear markets with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment during a recovery phase indicates synchronized supply and labor expansion, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in >55% of observations, or the joint effect loses predictive accuracy with an AUC below 0.52 in out-of-sample validation.
